title: Utah U.S. House District 4 election, 2026
description: This race is for Utah’s 4th Congressional District seat in the U.S. House of Representatives, with the general election set for Nov. 3, 2026. It matters because all 435 House seats are on the ballot and the outcome will affect control of the chamber. Utah’s court-ordered 2026 congressional map reshaped the district, making the Republican primary the main contest. The field includes incumbent Republican Mike Kennedy; Republican primary challengers Pasitale Lupeamanu and Seth Stewart; Democratic challenger Archie Williams III; and independent Steven Burt.
polling_note: No public polling was found for this race as of 2026-04-02.

candidates.name: Mike Kennedy
candidates.summary: Mike Kennedy is a Republican member of the U.S. House representing Utah’s 3rd Congressional District and is running for the Republican nomination in Utah’s 4th Congressional District in 2026. Before Congress, he served in the Utah Legislature and worked as a family doctor and attorney.

summary_sources.title: Mike Kennedy - Utah Declaration of Candidacy

career_history.title: Physician
career_history.description: Medical doctor; campaign and biography materials describe him as a family doctor.

career_history.title: Attorney
career_history.description: Practiced law in addition to medicine.

career_history.title: State legislator
career_history.description: Served in the Utah State Legislature before election to Congress.

candidates.donor_summary: Mike Kennedy’s federal campaign committee reported $610,158.88 in total receipts for 2025, including $574,063.88 in contributions and $290,500 from other committees. OpenSecrets shows his 2023–2024 PAC money was led by ideological/single-issue PACs ($136,850), followed by health ($58,500) and finance/insurance/real estate ($38,500), with business PACs slightly outweighing ideological PACs overall.
candidates.voting_summary: Kennedy is a sitting U.S. House member, so he has a legislative voting record. GovTrack shows he has voted mostly with the House Republican majority on recent roll calls, including support for AGOA extension and opposition to several appropriations and preparedness measures; he missed 12 of 470 votes from Jan. 2025 to Mar. 2026 (2.6%).

links.title: MIKE KENNEDY FOR UTAH - committee overview - FEC
Congressman Mike Kennedy | Representing Utah's 3rd Congressional District
Mike Kennedy - Utah Declaration of Candidacy

Economy.stance: Mike Kennedy advocates a pro-growth, fiscally conservative approach to the economy, prioritizing lower taxes, shrinking red tape for businesses, and policies aimed at reducing costs for families and supporting small-business growth. He emphasizes making life affordable and economic opportunity through limited government and accountable budgeting.

sources.title: Utah Small Businesses Endorse Mike Kennedy for Election to Congress - NFIB

Education.stance: Kennedy supports expanding federal resources for technical and career-technical education by broadening eligibility for existing grant programs to qualified technical colleges through the TECH Act, arguing this levels the playing field with traditional four-year institutions. He has also backed bipartisan efforts to boost rural STEM teaching in K-12 schools.

sources.title: Rep. Mike Kennedy's bill would boost funding at tech colleges – Deseret News

Climate/Energy.stance: Kennedy supports expanding domestic energy production by streamlining federal permitting for energy projects, including the License to Drill Act, to reduce bureaucratic delays. He also promotes public lands access to facilitate energy development and recreation.

sources.title: Rep. Kennedy Leads Bill to Keep American Energy Permitting on Track
Rep. Kennedy Introduces Bipartisan Energy Legislation to Unleash American Energy
Reps. Kennedy and Crank Co-Lead Bill to Protect Access for Public Land Recreation

Tech & AI.stance: Kennedy supports technology accountability and workforce development: he introduced the Algorithm Accountability Act to hold social media platforms accountable for harms caused by their algorithms, and the TECH Act to level the playing field for technical colleges by expanding eligibility for existing federal grant programs, aiming to improve AI safety and tech workforce training.

sources.title: Utah Rep. Mike Kennedy introduces the Algorithm Accountability Act to protect families online
Rep. Mike Kennedy introduces the TECH Act to Level the Playing Field for Technical Colleges
Rep. Mike Kennedy's TECH Act would boost funding at tech colleges
Utah Rep. Mike Kennedy introduces 'Algorithm Accountability Act'

Immigration.stance: Kennedy supports tightening immigration enforcement by standardizing detention facilities nationwide to match the U.S. Marshals Service standards, ensuring consistent safety and care for detainees. He also emphasizes expanding local detention capacity and stronger federal-local cooperation to address resource gaps and improve enforcement.

sources.title: Rep. Kennedy Introduces Equal Detention Standards Act to Standardize Immigration Detention and Support Local Law Enforcement
H.R. 5585 - Equal Detention Standards Act of 2025

Guns & Safety.stance: Mike Kennedy positions himself as a defender of the Second Amendment and supports constitutional carry/expanded gun rights, emphasizing the need to protect firearm ownership and oppose federal gun restrictions.

sources.title: Mike Kennedy for Utah - Campaign Website
Mike Kennedy for Utah - Campaign site

Local Issues.stance: Mike Kennedy supports protecting Utah's public lands and increasing local access by backing legislation like the Public Lands Access Restoration Act and advocating for reform of federal land management to reduce bureaucracy.

sources.title: Public Lands - Congressman Mike Kennedy - House.gov
Kennedy co-leads legislation to protect Utah's public lands

candidates.name: Steven Burt
candidates.summary: Steven Burt is an independent candidate for Utah’s 4th Congressional District in the 2026 U.S. House election. He is an attorney who previously practiced at Ballard Spahr, spent more than nine years in in-house roles at solar energy companies, and has an active pro bono practice.
candidates.donor_summary: Steven Burt’s campaign finance profile on Ballotpedia reports $19,549 in contributions and $12,619 in expenditures for his 2026 U.S. House campaign. The available disclosures reviewed do not show a dominant PAC or industry source of support.
candidates.voting_summary: Steven Burt is not a current or former legislator in the sources reviewed, so there is no legislative voting record to summarize. His public campaign materials instead describe his background in law, solar-energy policy, and pro bono immigration-related work.

links.title: Here's who is running for each of Utah's 4 congressional seats
Here's who is running for Congress in Utah - Deseret News

Economy.stance: Burt says he favors a pro-growth, free-market approach that reduces regulatory burdens and taxes to lower the cost of living and support small-business growth. He frames his campaign as independent and people-powered rather than party-driven.

Education.stance: Burt has not publicly released a formal education policy in the sources reviewed. His campaign materials emphasize independence and energy policy rather than education-specific proposals.

Healthcare.stance: No publicly stated healthcare policy was identified in the sources reviewed. Burt’s campaign materials emphasize independence and energy policy rather than healthcare.

Reproductive Rights.stance: No publicly stated position on reproductive rights was identified in the sources reviewed. Burt’s campaign materials emphasize independence and energy policy, with immigration issues mentioned but no abortion stance disclosed.

Climate/Energy.stance: Burt advocates for common-sense energy policy that supports solar energy and energy innovation through market-based approaches, emphasizing domestic energy independence and jobs in the energy sector.

Tech & AI.stance: Burt says current policy does not adequately address the risks posed by artificial intelligence and calls for action to protect people, systems, and the economy from AI-related threats.

Immigration.stance: Burt’s campaign materials do not publish a detailed immigration platform, but he says he has done pro bono representation associated with immigration issues. His public materials emphasize independence and broader policy themes rather than a specific immigration plan.

Foreign Policy.stance: Burt has not published a formal foreign policy platform in the sources reviewed. His campaign materials focus on independence and domestic policy areas such as energy and the economy.

sources.title: Vote Steven Burt for Congress - Home

Guns & Safety.stance: No explicit policy positions on guns or gun safety were identified in the sources reviewed. Burt’s campaign materials emphasize independence and general policy themes without specific gun-control or safety proposals.

Social Justice.stance: No publicly stated position on social justice was identified in the sources reviewed. Burt’s public materials emphasize independence and policy areas such as energy and immigration rather than a social-justice platform.

sources.title: Vote Steven Burt for Congress - Home

Election Reform.stance: Burt has not published a specific election reform platform in the sources reviewed. His campaign emphasizes independence and reducing partisanship but does not set out concrete election reform proposals.

Local Issues.stance: No explicit local-issues policy was identified in the sources reviewed. Burt’s materials emphasize unaffiliated independence and national priorities such as energy policy rather than Utah-specific local proposals.

sources.title: Vote Steven Burt for Congress - stevenburt.com

flags.concern: Contradictory district assignment: The summary states Kennedy 'is running for the Republican nomination in Utah's 4th Congressional District' (correct per the race), but also says he 'is a Republican member of the U.S. House representing Utah's 3rd Congressional District' (outdated). The House.gov link metadata reinforces this error ('Congressman Mike Kennedy | Representing Utah's 3rd Congressional District'). His career_history correctly lists the 4th since 2025, indicating redistricting occurred, but the summary reflects pre-redistricting language.
flags.suggestion: Revise the summary to: 'Mike Kennedy is a Republican member of the U.S. House representing Utah's 4th Congressional District (as of 2026 redistricting) and is seeking re-election in 2026. Before his current House service, he served in the Utah Legislature and worked as a family doctor and attorney.' Update the House.gov link metadata title or add a clarifying note about redistricting.

flags.concern: The image URL appears to reference a professional basketball player (Steven Burtt Jr.) rather than Steven Burt, the independent political candidate. This is a factual error that misrepresents the candidate.
flags.suggestion: Remove the image_url entirely and set to null, or replace with a verified campaign photo of Steven Burt. If no verified image is available, document this as 'No verified campaign image available as of 2026-04-02.'

flags.concern: The image URL points to Archie Williams High School (a building in California), not a portrait of candidate Archie Williams III. This is a factual mismatch.
flags.suggestion: Remove the image_url entirely and set to null, or replace with a verified candidate portrait. If no verified image is available, document this limitation.

flags.concern: The career_history array is empty despite the summary stating Burt 'spent more than nine years in in-house roles at solar energy companies' and 'previously practiced at Ballard Spahr' and 'has an active pro bono practice.' The Ballard Spahr law firm bio is cited in sources but the professional history is not structured in the profile.
flags.suggestion: Populate career_history with entries: (1) Attorney, Ballard Spahr LLP (private practice); (2) Solar Energy Sector (in-house counsel, 9+ years); (3) Pro Bono Counsel (immigration-related work). Reference the Ballard Spahr bio and campaign website.

flags.concern: The education array is empty. Burt's professional roles (attorney, in-house counsel) imply a law degree and undergraduate degree, but these are not documented in the profile.
flags.suggestion: Research and add education entries (law school, undergraduate institution, degree awarded) or explicitly document that education details are not publicly available.

flags.concern: The career_history array is empty despite Ballotpedia identifying Williams as 'a heavy equipment operator.' The summary references this occupation but does not structure it in the profile's career history.
flags.suggestion: Add a career_history entry: Heavy Equipment Operator (organization and dates if available). Reference Ballotpedia and Vote Smart.

flags.concern: The education array is empty. Ballotpedia explicitly states Williams has 'a bachelor's degree from Salt Lake Baptist College,' but this is not reflected in the profile.
flags.suggestion: Add education entry: Bachelor's degree, Salt Lake Baptist College (field if known). This is a straightforward factual addition.

flags.concern: The career_history array is empty for Pasitale Lupeamanu. While public information is limited (Ballotpedia notes the candidate has not completed their survey), no professional background is documented.
flags.suggestion: Research additional sources (news articles, social media, local community records) to populate career history. If minimal information is found, add a note: 'Limited professional background information publicly available as of 2026-04-02.'

flags.concern: The education array is empty for Lupeamanu. No public education records are documented.
flags.suggestion: Research education background or document that this information is not publicly available.

flags.concern: The career_history is incomplete. The summary identifies Stewart as 'a software engineer and AI/ML researcher,' but the career_history array only lists generic entries ('City council candidate' and 'Software engineer') without specifics. The AI/ML researcher role is absent, and the software engineer entry lacks company name or dates.
flags.suggestion: Add or expand entries: (1) AI/ML Researcher (organization/dates if publicly known); (2) Software Engineer (add company name if available, with dates). Reference the American Fork city document and campaign website.

flags.concern: The education array is empty for Seth Stewart. Given his roles as a software engineer and AI/ML researcher, post-secondary credentials (computer science degree, etc.) are likely, but none are documented.
flags.suggestion: Research Stewart's educational background or explicitly document that this information is not publicly available.

flags.field: candidates[1].issues (Healthcare, Reproductive Rights, Immigration, Foreign Policy, Guns & Safety, Social Justice, Election Reform, Local Issues)
flags.concern: Eight of Steven Burt's 12 issue categories have 'low' confidence with minimal sourcing and generic 'no publicly stated position' language. By contrast, Mike Kennedy has 'high' confidence across 7 major issues with detailed sourcing. This asymmetry creates an appearance of unequal profiling, even though it may reflect real differences in candidate disclosure.
flags.suggestion: Add a transparency note in the race-level description or candidate overview: 'Issue coverage varies significantly across candidates based on public disclosure of positions. Candidates vary in detail and accessibility of policy statements; gaps may reflect candidate communication choices rather than data collection limitations.' Conduct additional research to identify any Burt statements on healthcare or reproductive rights (e.g., social media, interviews, questionnaire responses).

flags.concern: All 12 issue stances for Archie Williams III are marked 'low' confidence with nearly identical language: 'No publicly stated position identified' or 'has not published a formal [policy area] platform.' This near-total absence of policy documentation is unusual and suggests either minimal campaign activity or insufficient research effort.
flags.suggestion: Conduct targeted research: (1) Visit Williams' campaign website (standfororem.org/archie-williams-iii) to extract any policy statements; (2) Search news archives (KSL, Deseret News, Salt Lake Tribune) for interviews or candidate forum coverage; (3) Check FEC finance filings for candidate contact information to request positions; (4) Review Vote Smart and Ballotpedia comments for any voter-submitted responses. If truly minimal disclosure exists after this effort, add a candidate-level note explaining the limitation rather than leaving readers with 12 identical 'low' entries.

flags.concern: Pasitale Lupeamanu has no 'high' confidence issue positions. Most entries are 'low' or 'medium' confidence, with heavy reliance on Ballotpedia's note that the candidate 'has not yet completed' their survey. No official campaign website is listed, and no campaign materials are directly sourced.
flags.suggestion: Conduct additional research: (1) Search for an official campaign website, social media accounts, or press releases; (2) Review local Utah news for any coverage; (3) Check FEC records for campaign committee contact information. If limited public information exists after this effort, add a candidate-level note acknowledging the gap, as recommended for Williams.

flags.concern: The confidence level is marked 'high' with the primary source being a Utah legislative minutes snippet (HB 76 testimony). However, this does not constitute a formal campaign policy statement; the stance appears inferred from legislative testimony on data-center regulation rather than directly articulated campaign messaging.
flags.suggestion: Downgrade confidence to 'medium' and revise the stance language to clarify: 'Based on his legislative testimony on HB 76 (data-center regulation), Stewart appears to support balanced regulation of technology, though he has not published a comprehensive campaign platform on AI or tech policy.' This avoids overstating the confidence level.

flags.concern: Pronoun inconsistency: the stance text refers to Pasitale Lupeamanu as 'her,' but earlier sections and standard convention refer to 'him.' Verify the candidate's pronouns.
flags.suggestion: Confirm the candidate's correct pronouns and ensure consistency throughout the profile. If the candidate uses 'her/she,' update all references; if 'he/him,' correct this stance field.

flags.concern: The description reads awkwardly: 'The field includes incumbent Republican Mike Kennedy; Republican primary challengers Pasitale Lupeamanu and Seth Stewart; Democratic challenger Archie Williams III; and independent Steven Burt.' While accurate, the phrasing could mislead readers about whether all five candidates are accounted for in the profiling or whether additional candidates exist.
flags.suggestion: Revise for clarity: 'This race is for Utah's 4th Congressional District seat in the U.S. House of Representatives, with the general election set for Nov. 3, 2026. The field consists of five candidates: incumbent Republican Mike Kennedy, Republican primary challengers Pasitale Lupeamanu and Seth Stewart, Democratic challenger Archie Williams III, and independent Steven Burt.'

flags.concern: The summary mixes two distinct reporting periods without clear temporal boundaries: '2025 total receipts' ($610,158.88) are followed immediately by '2023–2024 PAC money' data. While both are accurate, the temporal blending could confuse readers about the most current fundraising status.
flags.suggestion: Revise for clarity: 'Through year-end 2025, Kennedy's federal campaign committee reported $610,158.88 in total receipts, including $574,063.88 in contributions and $290,500 from other committees. For the 2023–2024 cycle, OpenSecrets data shows his PAC support was led by ideological/single-issue PACs ($136,850), followed by health ($58,500) and finance/insurance/real estate ($38,500).' This makes reporting periods explicit.

flags.concern: The note states 'No public polling was found for this race as of 2026-04-02' but does not clarify whether no polls have been conducted, polls exist but are unpublished, or polling is expected later.
flags.suggestion: Expand to: 'As of April 2, 2026, no publicly released polling was found for this race. Polling activity may increase closer to the June 23 primary and November 3 general election dates.'

flags.concern: Social media object is empty {} despite Burt having a campaign website and a professional background. This may be accurate, but should be explicitly documented.
flags.suggestion: If Burt has no public social media accounts, add a note: 'No public social media accounts identified as of 2026-04-02.' If accounts exist (Facebook, X, LinkedIn), populate them.

flags.concern: Social media object is empty for Archie Williams III, consistent with the overall lack of campaign visibility.
flags.suggestion: Search for Williams social media accounts (Facebook, X, Instagram). If none exist, document: 'No public social media accounts identified as of 2026-04-02.'

flags.concern: Website field is null for Pasitale Lupeamanu despite being a major-party (Republican) primary candidate. This is either a data gap or reflects lack of a campaign website.
flags.suggestion: Search for Lupeamanu's official campaign website. If none exists, document: 'No campaign website identified as of 2026-04-02.' If one exists, add the URL.

flags.concern: Social media object is empty for Seth Stewart despite him having a campaign website (sethandrewstewart.com).
flags.suggestion: Research Stewart's social media presence (Facebook, X, Instagram, LinkedIn) and populate if accounts exist. If not, document: 'No public social media accounts identified as of 2026-04-02.'

flags.concern: The profile lists five AI models as generators with version numbers that do not correspond to publicly known releases as of April 2, 2026: 'gpt-5.4-mini,' 'gpt-5-nano,' 'claude-haiku-4-5-20251001,' 'gemini-3.1-flash-lite-preview,' and 'grok-3-mini.' These version strings appear speculative or inaccurate and undermine confidence in the profile's metadata.
flags.suggestion: Verify and update the generator array to reflect actual models and versions used. If this profile was generated by a known tool or multiple tools, document the accurate tooling. If the versions are correct, provide context for their release dates or capabilities.

flags.concern: Two issue stances for Mike Kennedy are marked 'To be determined' with low confidence, creating an incomplete profile for the incumbent. By contrast, seven other major issues have high confidence.
flags.suggestion: Research Kennedy's website, press releases, and voting record to identify any statements on social justice (criminal justice reform, civil rights) and election reform (voting access, redistricting). If no clear stance exists, update the confidence note to clarify this represents a genuine lack of public position rather than incomplete data collection.

reviews.summary: This profile demonstrates excellent sourcing and depth for the incumbent candidate, Mike Kennedy, with comprehensive issue coverage backed by official congressional sources, campaign materials, and credible outlets. However, the profile has critical factual errors (image URLs pointing to wrong subjects, district representation confusion), significant coverage asymmetry (three challengers with minimal policy documentation and low-confidence entries), and incomplete biographical data (missing career histories and education for most non-incumbents). These issues—particularly the factual errors and the stark imbalance between the incumbent and challengers—require correction before publication to ensure accuracy and fairness.

flags.concern: Contradictory district representation (references 3rd District while the profile is for the 4th).
flags.suggestion: Update to consistently reflect CD-4 status.

flags.suggestion: Remove the invalid image URL and set to null.

flags.suggestion: Remove the invalid image URL and set to null.

flags.suggestion: Clarify that there are three Republican candidates in the primary alongside a Democrat and an independent.

flags.concern: The listed AI models (e.g., gpt-5.4-mini) are not verifiable as public versions as of April 2026.
flags.suggestion: Verify and correct the generator metadata to reflect accurate tools used.

reviews.summary: The profile is well-researched regarding the incumbent, Mike Kennedy, but requires significant revision to correct factual errors in candidate metadata and imagery. Specifically, the profile contains incorrect image references for challengers, contradictory district information, and substantial gaps in career and education histories for non-incumbents. The stark contrast in issue-category confidence levels suggests a need for more diligent sourcing regarding the challengers to ensure a balanced, neutral representation of the full field.

flags.concern: The summary incorrectly references Mike Kennedy as representing Utah's 3rd Congressional District, contradicting his career_history (which lists the 4th since 2025) and official sources, indicating a factual inaccuracy likely from outdated redistricting information.
flags.suggestion: Update the summary to consistently reflect his current representation of the 4th District and cross-reference with Congress.gov for accuracy.

flags.concern: The image URL incorrectly links to a professional basketball player named Steven Burtt Jr., rather than the candidate Steven Burt, which is a factual error and unrelated.
flags.suggestion: Replace with a verified campaign photo of Steven Burt or set to null if unavailable.

flags.concern: The image URL points to an image of Archie Williams High School, not a portrait of candidate Archie Williams III, representing a factual mismatch.
flags.suggestion: Remove or replace with a verified candidate image.

flags.concern: The array is empty despite the summary detailing Steven Burt's 20 years of legal practice and solar industry work, leaving a gap in completeness.
flags.suggestion: Populate with entries based on sources like his Ballard Spahr bio, including his attorney role and solar energy positions.

flags.concern: The array is empty, even though sources describe Archie Williams III as a heavy equipment operator with prior campaign experience, resulting in incomplete biographical coverage.
flags.suggestion: Add relevant entries from Ballotpedia and Vote Smart sources to include his professional background.

flags.concern: Most issue stances for Pasitale Lupeamanu have low confidence and rely on limited sources like Ballotpedia, with no official campaign website, leading to incomplete and potentially biased representation compared to the incumbent.
flags.suggestion: Incorporate additional sources, such as news interviews or campaign statements, to improve confidence and balance coverage across candidates.

flags.concern: The array is empty for Seth Stewart, despite his summary describing him as a software engineer and AI/ML researcher, which typically involves educational credentials, indicating a completeness gap.
flags.suggestion: Research and add education details from reliable sources or note their unavailability explicitly.

flags.concern: While Kennedy's issues are well-covered with high confidence, the profile's uneven depth across candidates (e.g., many 'low' confidence entries for others) may introduce unintentional bias toward the incumbent.
flags.suggestion: Ensure balanced sourcing and detail for all candidates by adding notes on data limitations for challengers, promoting fairness in presentation.

flags.concern: The listed AI models (e.g., gpt-5.4-mini) do not correspond to publicly known releases as of April 2026, potentially misrepresenting the profile's generation method and affecting source quality transparency.
flags.suggestion: Verify and update the generator array to accurately reflect the models used, or document the actual generation process.

reviews.summary: This profile provides strong, well-sourced details for the incumbent candidate, Mike Kennedy, with high-confidence issue positions and credible official sources, but it requires revisions due to factual inconsistencies (e.g., district representation errors), incomplete biographical data for non-incumbent candidates, and uneven coverage that may inadvertently favor the incumbent, highlighting gaps in career histories, education, and issue stances.
